title: Stateful Counter
description:
The counter refuses to count. Click `+` ten times and the display stays at 0 forever.

The developer used a plain variable instead of React state. React only re-renders when state changes — a plain `let` variable is invisible to it.

Fix the component so it uses `useState`:
- Clicking `+` increments the count by 1
- Clicking `-` decrements, but never below 0
- The displayed count updates on every click
objectives: Call `setCount(Math.max(0, count - 1))` in the decrement handler
hints: React only re-renders when state changes. Plain variables are invisible to React.
useState returns a pair: `[value, setter]`. Destructure it.
Never mutate state directly — always call the setter, e.g. `setCount(count + 1)`.
